question_frequency.rationale: The Pair presented two explicit forked decisions (start at 2p vs p², then full vs odd-only sieve) at meaningful points, engaging the user with didactic choices on most major design decisions.

question_quality.rationale: The first fork (2p vs p²) hits a genuine algorithmic insight central to the sieve. The second (odd-only optimization) raises a real engineering trade-off and the Pair commendably pushed back on premature optimization, surfacing the clarity-vs-performance tension.

scaffolding.rationale: Logical progression from core algorithm correctness to optimization trade-offs. The pushback in turn 3 was well-timed pedagogy. Minor weakness: the bytearray subtlety was raised but not resolved cleanly.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Natural back-and-forth, with the Pair appropriately challenging the student's optimization instinct rather than just accepting it. Tone is collegial and the conversation flows well.

effectiveness.rationale: Final code correctly produces 142,913,828,922. Uses a list of bools rather than bytearray with slice assignment (the baseline's faster approach), so slightly less optimized than baseline but functionally equivalent and arguably clearer.

friction.rationale: 4 turns vs baseline's 3, comparable wall time (72s vs 84s). The extra turn delivered real pedagogical value via the optimization-vs-clarity discussion, justifying the slight overhead.
